industries.name: Galvanizing & Corrosion Protection
uses: Hot-dip galvanizing: steel articles immersed in molten zinc at 450°C form a metallurgical zinc-iron alloy layer + outer pure zinc coating — provides sacrificial cathodic protection (zinc corrodes preferentially, protecting underlying steel) with 50–100+ year service life

Continuous hot-dip galvanizing (galvanized sheet steel, GI and Galvalume): steel strip passes through zinc or Zn-Al alloy bath at 180m/min — produces coated coil for automotive body panels, roofing, appliances, HVAC, and structural sections

Electrogalvanizing: electrodeposited zinc on steel strip for automotive exposed panels (hoods, doors, fenders) requiring a smoother, thinner coating than hot-dip — used by Toyota, Honda, and BMW for Class-A surface appearance

Zinc-aluminum-magnesium (ZM) coatings (SuperDyma, ZM310, MagiZinc): advanced alloy coatings replacing conventional GI on automotive and construction steel — superior cut-edge protection and formability with 30–40% less coating weight for equivalent corrosion performance
Batch galvanizing of structural steel fabrications, rebar, poles, guard rails, pipes, and hardware — provides decades of maintenance-free corrosion protection for infrastructure exposed to weather

companies.name: ArcelorMittal
companies.use: World's largest steel producer and major producer of galvanized sheet for automotive, construction, and appliance markets. Its Usibor and Ductibor hot-stamped zinc-coated steel are used in automotive safety structures globally.

companies.name: POSCO
companies.use: Major galvanized and Zn-Mg coated sheet producer. Supplies zinc-coated automotive and construction steel to Korean OEMs (Hyundai, Kia) and regional markets. Operates continuous galvanizing lines at Pohang and Gwangyang.

companies.name: AZZ (formerly Galvatech)
companies.use: Largest North American hot-dip batch galvanizing company. Processes structural steel fabrications, poles, and hardware for construction, utility, and transportation infrastructure customers across the US and Canada.

industries.name: Zinc Alloys & Die Casting
uses: Zamak die-cast alloys (Zn-4%Al-1%Cu, Zn-4%Al): produced by hot-chamber die casting at high rates into complex, thin-walled shapes for automotive parts (door handles, lock bodies, fuel system components), consumer goods, and electronic connectors

Brass (Cu-Zn): zinc strengthens and hardens copper; brasses range from 5% Zn (gilding metal for jewelry) to 40% Zn (Muntz metal, cartridge brass for shell casings). Used for plumbing fittings, musical instruments, valves, and architectural hardware

Zinc pressure die castings for automotive: mirror housings, brackets, carburetor bodies, and dashboard components — die casting's tight tolerances and complex geometries reduce machining costs vs. aluminum
Nickel silver (Cu-Ni-Zn, 'German silver'): used for cutlery, musical instrument valves, zippers, and optical frames — silver appearance without precious metal cost
Zinc-aluminum alloys (ZA-8, ZA-12, ZA-27) for gravity and pressure die casting of bearings, bushings, and wear-resistant engineering components

companies.name: Norsk Hydro (Precision Profiles)
companies.use: Produces zinc die-casting alloys and zamak master alloys for automotive, consumer goods, and electronics die casters. Supplies ready-to-use alloy ingots meeting tight compositional tolerances for consistent castability.

companies.name: Dynacast
companies.share: ~8% of global zinc die casting market
companies.use: World's largest precision zinc and aluminum die casting company. Produces zinc die-cast components for automotive (fuel system, trim), consumer electronics, and healthcare device industries at 23 global manufacturing sites.

companies.name: Illinois Tool Works (ITW)
companies.use: Operates zinc die casting operations within its Automotive OEM Segment. Produces zinc alloy fasteners, clips, and precision castings for automotive body-in-white and powertrain assembly applications.

industries.name: Rubber, Chemicals & Agriculture
uses: Zinc oxide (ZnO) as activator in rubber vulcanization — typically 3–5 parts ZnO per 100 parts rubber activates sulfur vulcanization by forming zinc stearate soap that accelerates cure. Every tire contains significant ZnO

Zinc oxide in ceramics, glass, and electronic components: varistors (ZnO surge protection), phosphors in fluorescent lights and LED phosphors, piezoelectric sensors, and UV-absorbing sunscreen (ZnO nanoparticles)

Zinc sulfate and zinc chelates as micronutrient fertilizer — zinc deficiency is the most widespread micronutrient deficiency in world agricultural soils, particularly in rice, wheat, and corn on alkaline soils
Zinc phosphate as primary anti-corrosion pigment in industrial primers and marine coatings — non-toxic replacement for red lead and chromate primers in structural steel and automotive coating systems
Zinc stearate and zinc carboxylates as lubricants and release agents in rubber and plastic processing, and as PVC heat stabilizers (zinc-calcium systems replacing lead-based stabilizers)

companies.name: Hindustan Zinc (Vedanta)
companies.use: World's largest integrated zinc producer. Operates zinc-lead mines (Sindesar Khurd, Rampura Agucha) in Rajasthan, India and zinc smelters at Chanderiya and Dariba. Major producer of zinc oxide for rubber, chemicals, and fertilizer.

companies.name: Sherwin-Williams
companies.share: ~17% of US architectural and industrial coatings market
companies.use: Major consumer of zinc phosphate anti-corrosion pigments in its industrial maintenance, marine, and protective coatings. Zinc-rich primers in its Zinc Clad product line provide cathodic protection equivalent to hot-dip galvanizing.

companies.name: Lanxess
companies.use: Produces rubber chemicals including zinc-based vulcanization accelerators and zinc oxide for the global tire and rubber goods industry. Supplies ZnO and zinc dithiocarbamates to tire manufacturers globally.

industries.name: Batteries & Emerging Energy Storage
uses: Alkaline batteries (AA, AAA, C, D): zinc powder anode in potassium hydroxide electrolyte — zinc is oxidized (Zn → Zn²⁺) while MnO₂ cathode is reduced. Each AA battery contains ~3.5g of zinc powder
Zinc-carbon (Leclanché) dry cells: zinc can serves simultaneously as container and anode — low-cost primary battery for low-drain applications in developing markets

Zinc-air batteries: ambient air provides oxygen at the cathode while zinc anode oxidizes — extremely high energy density (energy density per kg rivals lithium) used in hearing aids and emerging grid storage

Aqueous zinc-ion batteries: non-flammable, low-cost rechargeable batteries using zinc metal anode and aqueous electrolyte — promising for stationary grid storage where lithium-ion's flammability is a concern

Zinc nickel oxide batteries: rechargeable, non-toxic, water-based chemistry for grid-scale storage — EnZinc and several startups developing zinc as a sustainable alternative to lithium for long-duration storage

companies.name: Duracell (Berkshire Hathaway)
companies.use: World's leading alkaline battery brand. Each AA battery contains ~3.5g zinc powder anode. Duracell alone consumes tens of thousands of tonnes of zinc powder annually for its global battery production.

companies.name: Energizer Holdings
companies.use: Major alkaline and zinc-air battery manufacturer. Produces Energizer alkaline batteries and Rayovac zinc-air hearing aid batteries — the largest zinc-air battery market segment currently in commercial production.

companies.name: EOS Energy Enterprises
companies.use: Developing aqueous zinc-based rechargeable batteries (Znyth technology) for long-duration grid energy storage (3–12 hours). Uses zinc in an alkaline electrolyte as a low-cost, non-flammable alternative to lithium-ion for stationary storage.
